name: New Horizons
first: First flyby of Pluto and its moons; sharpest images of a Kuiper belt world
description: A nine-year sprint after the fastest launch to date, New Horizons compressed decades of anticipation into one July 2015 day — a world turned from a pixelated blur into mountains of water ice and nitrogen glaciers on Pluto's heart-shaped Tombaugh Regio. The spacecraft later flew Arrokoth, linking the mission to the primordial solar system. Flight arcs in Orrery are direct-transfer teaching models, not the actual Jupiter gravity assist.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Atlas V 551 leaves Earth on a Jupiter-gravity-assist path to Pluto.

events.label: PLUTO FLYBY
events.note: Closest approach to Pluto; high-rate imaging and plasma data flood Earth.
